meta.name: Intelligence Gathering
meta.description: Research assistant missions for interview preparation, data collection, coding, analysis, and PI alignment.

tasks.title: Prepare Complete Interview Mission Kit
tasks.subject: Interview Prep
tasks.successCriteria: Protocol reviewed, probes highlighted, logistics confirmed, recording tested
tasks.recoveryMission: Confirm logistics and top 5 questions only

steps.action: Open the interview protocol and read it through completely (5 min)
Highlight key probes and potential follow-up questions in a different color (5 min)
Add transition notes between sections (e.g., 'Now let's talk about...') (3 min)
Confirm interview date, time, and Zoom/Teams link or location (3 min)
Send confirmation message to participant with reminders (3 min)
Test recording software (audio and video) with a 1-minute sample (5 min)
Prepare backup recording method (phone or second device) (2 min)
Create a note-taking template with participant ID, date, key fields (5 min)
Add interview to calendar with 15-minute buffer before and after (2 min)
Review IRB/ethics requirements for this interview (2 min)

tasks.notes: Always have a backup recording method. Test everything twice. Prepare a list of standard transitions ('Thank you for that insight...').

tasks.title: Transcribe and Clean One Interview Segment
tasks.subject: Transcription & Cleaning
tasks.successCriteria: 15 minutes of audio transcribed, anonymized, and formatted consistently
tasks.failureCondition: Transcript contains major gaps or identifiable information

steps.action: Open audio file and transcription software (Otter.ai, Express Scribe, or manual) (2 min)
Set up speaker labels (P = Participant, I = Interviewer) (2 min)
Transcribe 5 minutes of audio (20-25 min depending on method)
Take a 2-minute break to rest eyes and hands (2 min)
Transcribe next 5 minutes of audio (20-25 min)
Take another 2-minute break (2 min)
Transcribe final 5 minutes of audio (20-25 min)
Read through transcript and correct obvious errors (5 min)
Remove all identifiers: names, locations, specific dates (5 min)
Mark unclear sections with [inaudible min:ss] timestamps (3 min)
Save both raw and anonymized versions with consistent naming (2 min)

tasks.notes: Transcription is time-consuming. Use AI tools for first pass, but always manually verify accuracy. Take breaks every 20 minutes to maintain quality.

tasks.title: Code One Transcript Batch with Memo
tasks.subject: Coding
tasks.successCriteria: 10-15 pages coded with consistent code application and 200+ word memo
tasks.recoveryMission: Code 2 pages and write a 3-bullet memo

steps.action: Open transcript and your codebook side by side (2 min)
Review existing codes and definitions (5 min)
Code first 3 pages carefully, checking definitions each time (15 min)
Code next 3 pages, noting any new patterns (15 min)
Take a 3-minute break (3 min)
Code next 4 pages, marking ambiguous segments (15 min)
Review coded segments for consistency (10 min)
Write analytic memo: emerging patterns, surprises, tensions (15 min)
Note any new codes needed and add to codebook (5 min)
Save coded file and memo in analysis folder (3 min)

tasks.notes: Code in batches of 10-15 pages. Write memos immediately after coding while insights are fresh. Don't skip the memo - it's where analysis happens.
